zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Coleman於大約1657年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪時，她的父親Robert Coleman38歲，母親Elizabeth Grizzell26歲。她與Robert Tucker結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1722年12月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 喬治享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Coleman于大约1657年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪时，她的父亲Robert Coleman38岁，母亲Elizabeth Grizzell26岁。她与Robert Tucker结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1722年12月21日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州普林斯 喬治享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Coleman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Kalman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Gabor, Sandor, Imre, Lajos, Geza, Gyula, Katalin, Zoltan.

Hungarian (Kálmán): from the old Hungarian personal name Kálmán, meaning ‘remainder’ (from Turkic kal ‘to remain’), hence a protective name, which was given to infants to ward off evil and harmful spirits. In medieval times the name became particularly popular because it was confused with the German Christian name Koloman of Irish origin (see Kolman 2).

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Kalmen, an everyday form of Kloynemes (from Hebrew Kalonimos, which is from Greek kalos ‘lovely’ or kallos ‘beauty’ + onyma ‘name’). This Hebrew name is first recorded in the Talmud and has been used continuously since then. Among Hungarian Jews, it was sometimes confused with 1 above.

variants.name: Colman
meanings: Irish (Mayo and Sligo) and English: variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kohlmann   or   Kollmann  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Clifford
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Collman
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German   Kollmann  , or of   Kohlmann  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Coleman was born about 1657, in Charles City County, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Robert Coleman, was 38 and her mother, Elizabeth Grizzell, was 26. She married Robert Tucker. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 21 December 1722, in Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 66.

Elle a épousé Robert Tucker. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 décembre 1722, à Prince George, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 66 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Coleman(이)가 대략 1657년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 찰스 시티 카운티에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Coleman(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Grizzell(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Robert Tucker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1722년 12월 21일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 프린스 조지에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Coleman nasceu aproximadamente 1657, em Charles City County, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Robert Coleman, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Grizzell, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Tucker. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 21 de dezembro de 1722, em Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Coleman родилась около 1657 г. в местности чарльс сити коунти, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Robert Coleman было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Grizzell, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Tucker. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 декабря 1722 г. в местности принц джеордж, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Coleman nació aproximadamente 1657, en Charles City County, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Robert Coleman, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Grizzell, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Robert Tucker. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 21 de diciembre de 1722, en Prince George, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 66 años.
